_metadata.description: Doc Update Agent — reads cached source docs from source_docs/, diffs against templates, proposes additive improvements for human approval. Separate refresh step updates the cache.
_metadata.instructions: Load source files from primary_data.sources (each has a cache_file path). Diff cached content against templates. Never apply changes without per-proposal human approval. To refresh stale sources, fetch their URLs and overwrite their cache_file.
_metadata.usage: JSON = source config, diff rules, scoring, proposal schema. MD = philosophy, workflow, pitfalls. source_docs/ = one .md per platform source.

agent_details.description: Reads cached platform documentation from source_docs/ and surfaces additive improvements to repo templates with source citations, for human approval.

agent_type.description: Two-mode workflow: (A) Analysis — read cache → diff → score → propose → gate; (B) Refresh — fetch live docs → update cache files. LLM used for diff interpretation and proposal generation.

steps.name: Check Cache (Analysis Mode)
steps.description: Read each source's cache_file from source_docs/. Check the last_fetched date in the file header and fetch_status. Sources with fetch_status: 'success' and last_fetched within staleness_threshold_days are ready to use. Sources with 'failed' or 'not_attempted' status are noted in the run_summary but not blocking — analysis proceeds on available sources.
steps.output: list of available sources with content, list of unavailable sources for run_summary

steps.name: Refresh Cache (Refresh Mode — run separately)
steps.description: Fetch live content from source URLs for stale or failed sources. Overwrite the corresponding cache_file in source_docs/, updating last_fetched and fetch_status in the file header. Run this step independently before an analysis run when sources are known to be outdated.
steps.input: source URLs from primary_data.sources where cache is stale or failed
steps.output: updated cache_file(s) in source_docs/ with fresh content and new last_fetched date

steps.name: Load Source Content
steps.description: Read content from each available cache_file in source_docs/. Parse the metadata header (platform, label, source_url, last_fetched, fetch_status) and extract the body content for diffing.
steps.output: parsed source content per platform with metadata

steps.name: Load Target Templates
steps.description: Read current content of all target files listed in primary_data.update_targets.
steps.output: parsed content of make_agent.md, make_agent.json, make_gems/make_gem_qc.md, make_gems/make_gem_qc.json

steps.name: Diff and Detect Candidates
steps.description: Apply diff_rules to identify content in fetched docs not represented in templates. Filter out replace-type changes against non-placeholder content.
steps.output: list of candidate additions with target section and source reference

steps.name: Score Candidates
steps.description: Score each candidate using the scoring rubric. Apply convergence bonus for patterns appearing in 2+ platforms.
steps.output: scored and ranked proposal list, filtered by min_threshold

steps.name: Generate Proposals
steps.description: Format each scored candidate as a structured change proposal using proposal_template. Include source URL, platform, rationale, target, proposed text.
steps.output: formatted proposal list sorted by score descending

steps.name: Present for Approval
steps.description: Output all proposals with numbering. Halt. Do not modify any files. Wait for explicit per-proposal approval.
steps.output: numbered proposal list displayed to human

steps.name: Apply Approved Changes
steps.description: For each approved proposal, apply the change to the target file and section. Append new entries; do not overwrite existing non-placeholder content.
steps.output: updated target files; changelog entry in update_agent.json

workflow_based.exit_point: Step 7: Apply Approved Changes (only after explicit human approval per proposal)

llm_agent.system_prompt: You are a documentation analyst specializing in AI agent and skill design patterns. Given fetched documentation content and the current state of template files, your job is to: (1) identify patterns, conventions, or best practices in the fetched docs that are absent from the templates, (2) assess whether each finding is genuinely additive rather than a rewrite of existing content, (3) generate a clear rationale for each proposal. You do NOT modify files. You only generate proposals. Every proposal must include the exact source URL.

tools.name: WebFetch
tools.description: Fetch live documentation pages from source URLs

tools.name: Read
tools.description: Read current template files for diffing

io_contract.description: Inputs are the live doc pages and current template files. Output is a proposal list; no files are modified until approval.

inputs.name: sources
inputs.format: List of {platform, url, section_focus} from primary_data.sources

inputs.name: target_files
inputs.format: File paths of templates to diff against

outputs.name: proposals
outputs.format: Array of change_proposal objects per proposal_template schema
outputs.conditions: Only proposals scoring >= min_threshold are included

outputs.name: run_summary
outputs.conditions: Always produced, even when zero proposals meet threshold

io_contract.side_effects: None until Step 7. Step 7 modifies target files only for approved proposals. Appends to changelog in update_agent.json.

primary_data.description: Source URLs, diff rules, scoring rubric, proposal template, and update targets

sources.label: Claude Agents Overview
sources.section_focus: agent patterns, tool use, memory, orchestration
sources.notes: Primary source for Claude-specific agent best practices. Currently 404 at redirect — see cache file for status.

sources.label: Tool Use Guide
sources.section_focus: tool_choice, fallback handling, error recovery, strict mode, parallel tool use, tool runner
sources.notes: Key source for tool use patterns in error_handling and implementation.llm_agent. Last fetch: 2026-03-11 success.

sources.label: Claude Agent SDK
sources.section_focus: agent SDK conventions, agent loop patterns, subagents, sessions, hooks, compaction
sources.notes: Renamed from Claude Code SDK. Last fetch: 2026-03-11 success.

sources.label: Gemini API Agents
sources.section_focus: Gemini agent capabilities, function calling, grounding, thinking models
sources.notes: Original URL 404d — content from function calling and thinking pages. Last fetch: 2026-03-11 partial.

sources.label: Gemini Structured Output
sources.section_focus: response_mime_type, response_json_schema, JSON schema enforcement, structured output vs function calling

sources.label: OpenAI Agents SDK
sources.section_focus: agent primitives (Agents, Handoffs, Guardrails), lifecycle hooks, multi-agent patterns, tool_choice, structured output, MCP
sources.notes: platform.openai.com returns 403. Using GitHub Pages docs instead — fully accessible. Last fetch: 2026-03-11 success.

sources.label: Grok API Overview
sources.section_focus: agent API patterns, function calling, system prompt conventions, tool_choice, parallel tools
sources.notes: Last fetch: 2026-03-11 partial (main overview minimal; supplemented with quickstart, function calling, models).

change_type_rules.add: Target section exists but is empty, a stub, or a template placeholder
change_type_rules.extend: Target section exists with real content; proposed addition is orthogonal and non-overlapping
change_type_rules.replace: BLOCKED — never propose replacements of existing non-placeholder content
change_type_rules.note: Proposals classified as 'replace' are silently dropped before scoring

scoring.description: Score each candidate addition 0-100 before presenting as a proposal

criteria.name: Absent from all target files
criteria.description: The pattern or practice is not represented in any section of any target file

criteria.name: Actionable
criteria.description: The proposed addition can be written as a concrete entry (a rule, a parameter, a principle) not a vague suggestion

criteria.name: Directly maps to eligible section
criteria.description: There is a clear target section in update_targets where this belongs

criteria.name: Source is official developer documentation
criteria.description: The source URL is on a developer docs domain, not a blog or marketing page

criteria.name: Recently added or updated in source docs
criteria.description: The content appears to be new or recently revised based on page metadata or content signals

convergence_bonus.description: Add bonus points when the same pattern appears independently in 2+ platform docs
convergence_bonus.note: Apply after per-source scoring, before threshold filtering

proposal_template.description: Schema for each change proposal presented to the human

fields.score: integer 0-100 — final score after convergence bonus
fields.platform: string|array — source platform(s) if convergence detected
fields.source_url: string|array — direct URL(s) to the documentation section
fields.fetched_date: ISO date — when the source was fetched
fields.target_file: string — which file this change applies to
fields.proposed_text: string|object — exact text or JSON to add
fields.rationale: string — one paragraph explaining why this is valuable and what gap it fills
fields.convergence_note: string|null — names the other platforms that corroborate this finding

proposed_text.action: Set tool_choice to 'none', switch to text-only response, log failure with tool name and error code

example.rationale: The template's error_handling.fallbacks array is empty. Anthropic's tool use docs explicitly recommend a fallback to text-only mode when tool calls fail after retries. This directly fills a gap in the template for llm_agent type agents.

apis: WebFetch (HTTP GET with no-cache) — Mode B only
data_sources: source_docs/ folder — 10 cached platform documentation files

optional_dependencies.description: WebFetch only needed for Mode B (Refresh). Mode A (Analysis) is fully offline.

validation.description: How to verify the update agent is working correctly
pre_run_checklist: All source URLs in primary_data.sources are reachable
All target files in primary_data.update_targets exist in the repo
No prior run has modified target files without approval
post_run_checklist: Every proposal includes a non-null source_url
Proposals are sorted by score descending
run_summary.sources_fetched equals the count of sources in primary_data.sources
No target files were modified before approval step
success_criteria: At least 1 proposal is generated when fetched docs contain patterns absent from templates
Zero proposals are generated when fetched docs contain only patterns already in templates
Convergence bonus correctly increases score for 2+ platform matches
No file modifications occur before Step 7
All proposals include source_url, platform, fetched_date, and rationale

test_cases.name: New pattern absent from all templates
test_cases.input: Mock fetched doc containing 'agent handoff protocol' — not present in any template
test_cases.expected_output: At least 1 proposal with target_section in update_targets, score >= 60

test_cases.name: Existing pattern already in templates
test_cases.input: Mock fetched doc containing content identical to existing make_agent.md Key Principles

test_cases.name: Convergence bonus applied
test_cases.input: Same pattern fetched from both OpenAI and Google docs
test_cases.expected_output: Single merged proposal with score >= base_score + 15, platform field is array of both

test_cases.name: Replace-type change blocked
test_cases.input: Mock fetched doc suggesting rewording of existing make_agent.md Mission section
test_cases.expected_output: Zero proposals — replace-type change filtered before scoring

test_cases.name: No files modified before approval
test_cases.expected_output: All target files are byte-identical to pre-run state at end of Step 6

tolerance.numeric: Score within ±5 of expected is acceptable
tolerance.string: Proposal text comparison: semantic match acceptable, not exact match required
tolerance.structural: All required fields present in proposal object

when_to_use: Periodic maintenance runs (e.g., monthly) to catch new platform doc updates
After a major model release from any of the four platforms
Before a significant revision of make_agent.* or make_gem_qc.* templates
When a new agent pattern has been heard about but not yet confirmed in docs
when_not_to_use: For validating individual agents — use make_agent_qc instead
For rewriting templates from scratch — this agent is additive only
For non-documentation sources (blog posts, X/Twitter threads, conference talks)
best_practices: Run no more than once per month to avoid noise from minor doc edits
Review proposals in score order; address highest-value additions first
When approving, always read the source_url directly to verify context before accepting
After each approved run, update changelog in this JSON with run_date and approved_count
If a proposal score is borderline (60-70), open the source URL and re-read before deciding
alternatives: Manual monthly review: bookmark the 10 source URLs and scan manually
RSS/changelog monitoring: some platforms publish doc changelogs

known_failures.cause: Doc page moved, rate limited, or platform outage
known_failures.action: Skip that source, log warning in run_summary, continue with remaining sources

known_failures.error: Fetched content is empty or malformed
known_failures.action: Log warning, skip source, note in run_summary that manual review of that URL is recommended

known_failures.cause: All detected patterns already in templates, or all sources unreachable
known_failures.action: Output run_summary with explanation; do not report false positives

fallbacks.action: Abort run, output error summary, recommend manual review of source URLs

error_handling.timeout_behavior: Skip source after timeout; continue with remaining
error_handling.error_reporting: Include all skipped sources and failure reasons in run_summary output

related_agents.name: make_agent_qc
related_agents.relationship: Validates the templates that this agent updates; run after applying approved proposals

related_agents.name: make_gem_qc
related_agents.relationship: Validates Gem definitions downstream of this agent's updates to make_gems/make_gem_qc.*

related_agents.name: make_agent
related_agents.relationship: Primary update target; template used by all new agents in this repo

changes: Added source_docs/ folder — 10 cached platform documentation files (one per source)
Each cache file has a YAML-like metadata header: platform, label, source_url, last_fetched, fetch_status, notes
Workflow split into Mode A (Analysis — reads cache) and Mode B (Refresh — fetches live, updates cache)
Added cache_file field to each source entry in primary_data.sources
Added staleness_threshold_days to primary_data for configuring how fresh cache must be
Mode A is now fully offline-capable; WebFetch only needed for Mode B
Updated update_agent.md quickstart, workflow steps, pitfalls, resources, and file organization sections
First analysis run completed: 9 proposals generated from Anthropic + Google + xAI sources
Initial version — 10 source URLs across Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI
Workflow: fetch → diff → score → propose → gate → apply
Convergence bonus for cross-platform pattern detection
